channel.title: Frontion — Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇫🇷 Trump arrives at G7 summit in France touting Iran deal, pushes to end Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump arrived at the G7 summit in the French Alps after announcing a preliminary US-Iran deal, now pressing allies to help end the Ukraine-Russia war as well.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Trump eyes ending Ukraine war after taking Iran deal to G7 leaders
item.description: After securing a preliminary Iran deal, Trump turned his focus to Ukraine at the G7, seeking momentum to push for an end to the war with Russia as leaders convened in France.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japanese shippers await mine clearance details before resuming Hormuz transit
item.description: Japanese shipping companies welcomed the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz but say they need details on mine clearance and security guarantees before sending vessels through the chokepoint.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK bans under-16s from social media apps including TikTok and YouTube
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a ban on social media for children under 16, covering apps like Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube, with the restrictions expected to take effect in early 2027.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France shuts down Israeli company stands at Eurosatory defense exhibition
item.description: A dozen Israeli company stands were shut down at the Eurosatory defense show outside Paris, sparking outrage from Israeli authorities and raising tensions over arms exports during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🛢️ France and Britain push multinational Hormuz naval mission as ceasefire nears
item.description: France and Britain are advancing plans for a multinational naval mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if a US-Iran ceasefire holds, though it remains unclear whether Tehran would accept any such foreign naval presence.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia slashes fuel quality standards as Ukrainian drone strikes squeeze refineries
item.description:
Russia is allowing refineries to produce fuel with lower environmental specifications for the domestic market, Kommersant reports, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have doubled since the start of 2026, forcing partial shutdowns and supply squeezes.
Kyiv Post: Russian gas stations in Moscow and St. Petersburg have introduced purchase limits — restricting customers to 20 liters of gasoline — following successful Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil refineries that disrupted domestic fuel supply chains.
Reuters: Both sides exchanged overnight drone attacks, with Ukraine striking oil refineries and a petrochemicals plant while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

item.title: ⚔️ US-Iran deal reached but key questions remain as Trump arrives at G7
item.description:
The US and Iran say they have reached an agreement set to take effect Friday, but critical questions about enforcement, monitoring, and the fate of regional allies remain unanswered. The breakthrough came just hours before Trump landed in France for the G7 summit.
CBS News: The US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel immediately declared it would not withdraw from southern Lebanon, while Iran insisted on receiving half of its frozen assets before final negotiations.
CNN: The preliminary US-Iran ceasefire agreement, announced hours before the G7 summit, leaves major unresolved issues including Iran's nuclear program, enforcement mechanisms, and the timeline for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan Says Turkey Chose Diplomacy Over Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: President Erdogan stated Turkey pursued a measured, diplomacy-first policy during the US-Iran conflict, positioning Turkey as a regional mediator as the Iran deal takes shape.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇮🇱 Israeli Defense Firms Booths Boarded Up at Eurosatory After French Ban
item.description:
France shut down Israeli exhibitor spaces at the Eurosatory defense trade show, boarding up booths of smaller Israeli firms while larger companies remain stripped of weapons displays, drawing sharp criticism from Israel’s Defense Ministry.
Straits Times: French authorities shut down a dozen Israeli company stands at the Eurosatory defense trade show outside Paris, drawing condemnation from Israel's ambassador who called the move unacceptable.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump, Vance, and Iran Speaker Ghalibaf Electronically Sign Peace Memo
item.description: A senior US official confirmed that President Trump, Vice President Vance, and Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf electronically signed the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with a formal ceremony planned for Friday in Geneva.

item.title: 🌍 G7 Allies Seek Clarity on Iran Deal as Trump Arrives in France
item.description: World leaders gathering at the G7 summit in France pressed for details on the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with questions remaining about enforcement, the Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline, and Israel-Hezbollah tensions that could threaten the deal.

item.title: 🇫🇷 G7 Summit Opens in France as Trump Arrives With Iran Deal and Tariff Threats
item.description:
World leaders gathered at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, where relief over the US-Iran peace deal was tempered by Trump’s new 100% tariff threats against French wines over a digital services tax.
AP News: President Trump arrived at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France, touting the newly signed US-Iran agreement and telling allies the deal will bring success for the world, while signaling he now wants to turn his attention to ending the Ukraine-Russia war.
The Guardian: In a friendly and frank phone call, Trump also said the US is nearing a peace deal with Iran, according to a Putin adviser.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israelis Across Political Spectrum Furious Over US-Iran Deal
item.description: Israelis from across the political spectrum are expressing anger at the interim US-Iran peace deal, calling it a disaster and putting pressure on Netanyahu as Israel vows to keep forces in Lebanon and Syria.

item.title: 🇸🇴 Somaliland Opens Embassy in Jerusalem After Israeli Recognition
item.description: Somaliland opened its embassy in Jerusalem, months after Israel became the first country to recognize the breakaway African state, in a move with significant geopolitical implications for the Horn of Africa.

item.title: 📈 Global Stocks Surge and Oil Prices Drop After US-Iran Deal
item.description: Shares soared worldwide and oil prices fell after the US and Iran reached a tentative deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, though analysts warn it could take weeks or months for oil flows to normalize.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 Ukraine officially launches EU membership negotiations amid ongoing war with Russia
item.description: Ukraine began formal EU accession talks on Monday, starting a years-long process of reforms even as it continues fighting Russia's invasion. The move marks a historic shift for Kyiv's geopolitical orientation.

item.title: 🇪🇶⚔️ EU Adopts New Russia Sanctions Targeting Energy Revenues and Propaganda
item.description: The Council of the EU approved a new sanctions package against Russia, adding 34 individuals and 47 entities, with a focus on energy revenue flows and state propaganda infrastructure supporting the invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: 🇺🇦💔 4 Rescuers Killed in Russian Double-Tap Strike on Kharkiv
item.description: Russian forces carried out a double-tap missile strike on Kharkiv, killing four rescue workers and injuring six others. The second missile hit approximately 30 seconds after the first, targeting emergency responders arriving at the scene.

item.title: 🇺🇦✈️ Economist Analysis: Ukraine Intensifying Air War Reflects Russia Ground Difficulties
item.description: The Economist reports that the escalating air war with mass drone and missile barrages is partly a reflection of Russia's increasing difficulties on the ground, as Ukraine adapts with new drone tactics and technology.

item.title: 🇪🇶🏛️ EU Sanctions Putins Confessor and Russias Prosecutor General in New Package
item.description: The EU added 34 individuals and 47 organizations to its sanctions list, including Putin's personal confessor and Russia's Prosecutor General, expanding pressure on Moscow's inner circle and propaganda apparatus.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Israel vows to stay 'indefinitely' in lands seized in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza
item.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said ground forces will remain indefinitely in territories captured in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza, potentially challenging the US-Iran ceasefire deal that hinges on regional de-escalation.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Strike Sets Fire to Historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Cathedral
item.description:
A massive overnight Russian drone and missile barrage on Kyiv killed at least 11 people and set fire to the 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery. Russia denied striking the cathedral, blaming Ukrainian air defense systems.
Reuters: A major Russian overnight attack sparked an intense blaze at the UNESCO-listed Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, a symbol of Ukrainian spiritual history founded in 1051. France compared the attack to bombing Notre Dame.

item.title: 🇬🇧 London court convicts two men in Russian-linked plot to firebomb properties tied to UK Prime Minister
item.description: Two men were convicted of an arson plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure known as "El Money" who offered payment via Telegram to torch houses and a car linked to Keir Starmer.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Drone strikes killed over 1,000 civilians in Sudan in first five months of 2026, UN rights chief says
item.description: A senior UN official reported that drone strikes have killed more than 1,000 civilians in war-torn Sudan since January, highlighting the devastating toll of the ongoing civil conflict between the SAF and RSF.
